description: Semantic layer for banks, insurers, investment firms, accounting teams, and corporate finance. Covers accounts receivable, cash flow, budgeting, GL reconciliation, and portfolio management.

entities.name: Account
entities.description: Financial account in the general ledger or client portfolio
entities.operational_meaning: Foundation for P&L, balance sheet, and client-level financial analytics

entities.name: Transaction
entities.description: A financial event recorded in the general ledger
entities.operational_meaning: Primary source for cash flow, reconciliation, and financial reporting

entities.name: Invoice
entities.description: A billing document for goods or services rendered
entities.operational_meaning: Central to AR aging, DSO, and collection analytics

entities.name: Payment
entities.description: A cash inflow or outflow recorded against an invoice or account
entities.operational_meaning: Used for cash flow, payment performance, and reconciliation

entities.name: Budget
entities.description: Planned or forecasted financial figures for a period
entities.operational_meaning: Used for variance analysis, budget vs actuals, and reforecasting

entities.name: Cost Centre
entities.description: Organisational unit responsible for a portion of costs
entities.operational_meaning: Used for departmental P&L, cost allocation, and responsibility accounting

entities.name: Client
entities.description: Organisation or individual with a financial relationship
entities.operational_meaning: Used for client-level profitability, exposure, and relationship metrics

vocabulary.definition: Classification of outstanding invoices by how long they have been unpaid
vocabulary.sql_hint: CURRENT_DATE - invoice_date or due_date bucketed into 0-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days

vocabulary.definition: Average number of days taken to collect payment after a sale
Invoice or payment that has passed its due date without being settled
vocabulary.sql_hint: due_date < CURRENT_DATE AND status NOT IN ('PAID','CLEARED','SETTLED','CLOSED')

vocabulary.definition: Difference between actual financial result and the budgeted amount
vocabulary.sql_hint: actual_amount - budget_amount (positive = over budget for costs)

vocabulary.definition: Rate at which cash or budget is being consumed in a period
vocabulary.sql_hint: SUM(expenses) / period_count for monthly or weekly rate

vocabulary.definition: Revenue earned or expense incurred but not yet received or paid
Process of matching transactions in two systems to identify discrepancies
vocabulary.sql_hint: unmatched_transactions WHERE bank_statement_id IS NULL or gl_entry_id IS NULL

vocabulary.definition: Current assets minus current liabilities — measure of operational liquidity

suggested_questions: What is the AR aging summary by client as of today?
Which invoices are overdue by more than 60 days?
Show me budget vs actuals by cost centre this quarter
What is the DSO trend by month for the past year?
Which clients have the highest outstanding balance right now?
Show me the cash flow forecast vs actual for this month
What is our current burn rate compared to budget?
Which accounts have unreconciled transactions this period?

kpi_definitions.name: Cash Conversion Cycle
kpi_definitions.formula_description: DSO + DIO - DPO (days inventory outstanding - days payable outstanding)

alert_templates.name: High Overdue Receivables
alert_templates.description: Overdue invoices have exceeded the risk threshold
alert_templates.metric_hint: SUM(outstanding_amount) FROM invoices WHERE due_date < CURRENT_DATE AND status != 'PAID'

alert_templates.name: Budget Overspend
alert_templates.description: A cost centre has exceeded its approved budget
alert_templates.metric_hint: SUM(actual_amount) - SUM(budget_amount) FROM financials GROUP BY cost_centre WHERE period = CURRENT_MONTH

alert_templates.name: Unreconciled Transactions
alert_templates.description: Number of unreconciled transactions has exceeded the threshold
alert_templates.metric_hint: COUNT(*) FROM transactions WHERE reconciliation_status = 'UNMATCHED' AND transaction_date < CURRENT_DATE - 7
